data.title: Write worktree isolation verification tests and document findings
data.description:
## Description

Create tests/hooks/test-worktree-isolation-verification.sh that verifies:

1. **Git root isolation**: Create a git worktree in a temp directory, run git rev-parse --show-toplevel, confirm it differs from the parent repo root
2. **Artifacts dir isolation**: Source plugins/dso/hooks/lib/deps.sh, set REPO_ROOT to the worktree path, call get_artifacts_dir(), confirm it produces a different path than when REPO_ROOT points to the parent repo
3. **Worktree cleanup**: Verify git worktree remove cleans up after the test

After all tests pass, record findings as a ticket comment on story 10bc-ccfb documenting:
- Confirmed/denied: worktree git root differs from parent
- Confirmed/denied: get_artifacts_dir() produces worktree-specific path via REPO_ROOT hash
- Observation: worktree retention behavior (framework-managed)
- Limitation: actual Agent dispatch with isolation:worktree cannot be tested until guard update (Story fb66-2a96)

Use existing test infrastructure (tests/lib/assert.sh).

### Files to modify
- tests/hooks/test-worktree-isolation-verification.sh (CREATE)

### TDD Exemption
Unit test exemption — criterion 1: no conditional logic. This spike verifies existing framework behavior. Tests ARE the deliverable, not a preceding RED dependency. No new behavioral code is written.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test file exists at tests/hooks/test-worktree-isolation-verification.sh
  Verify: test -f tests/hooks/test-worktree-isolation-verification.sh
- [ ] All tests pass (exit 0)
  Verify: bash tests/hooks/test-worktree-isolation-verification.sh
- [ ] Test file contains at least 3 test functions
  Verify: grep -c 'echo.*---.*test_' tests/hooks/test-worktree-isolation-verification.sh | awk '{exit ($1 < 3)}'
- [ ] Ticket comment with findings recorded on story 10bc-ccfb
  Verify: .claude/scripts/dso ticket show 10bc-ccfb | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); c=[x for x in d['comments'] if 'FINDINGS' in x['body']]; exit(0 if c else 1)"
